title: Texas Capital Bancshares Could Soar If These 2 Things Go Right
author: Jack Delaney
description: Texas Capital Bancshares has built diversified revenue streams through fee-based businesses including treasury services, wealth management, and investment banking, achieving record fee income for three consecutive years. The bank's mortgage finance segment is strengthening with enhanced credit structures and potential 15% balance growth in 2026. Trading at a reasonable 13.5 forward P/E ratio, the stock could see meaningful gains if the bank continues executing, though it offers no dividend.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The bank demonstrates strong execution with three consecutive years of record fee income, growing fee-based revenue to $229 million in 2025. Management forecasts $160-175 million in investment banking fees for 2026, and mortgage finance balances could grow 15%. The stock trades at a reasonable valuation (13.5 forward P/E) with room for upside if execution continues, though recent 5-year performance (40%) lags the S&P 500 (77%).

title: Texas Capital Bancshares, Inc. Announces Quarterly Dividend for Preferred Stock
author: Na
description: Texas Capital Bancshares declared a cash dividend of $14.375 per share of its 5.75% Fixed Rate Non-Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock, Series B, payable on March 16, 2026. The company reported strong fourth quarter 2025 results with net income up 44% year-over-year and record-level increases in book value and tangible book value per share.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company demonstrated strong financial performance with 44% year-over-year net income growth in Q4 2025, record-level increases in both book value and tangible book value per share (13% increase), and the ability to declare a dividend on preferred stock, all indicating solid operational and financial health.

title: Texas Capital Bancshares, Inc. Announces Quarterly Dividend for Preferred Stock
author: Globe Newswire
description: Texas Capital Bancshares (NASDAQ: TCBI) announced a quarterly cash dividend of $14.375 per share of its 5.75% Fixed Rate Non-Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock, Series B, equivalent to $0.359375 per depositary share (TCBIO). The dividend is payable on March 16, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 2, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is maintaining its dividend payments on preferred stock, demonstrating financial stability and commitment to returning capital to shareholders. Regular dividend announcements are generally viewed positively as they indicate operational health and investor confidence.

title: Texas Capital Bancshares, Inc. Announces Date for Full Year and Q4 2025 Operating Results
author: Na
description: Texas Capital Bancshares, Inc. (NASDAQ: TCBI) announced it will release its full year and fourth quarter 2025 financial results on January 22, 2026, before market open. Executive management will host a conference call and webcast at 9:00 a.m. EDT to discuss operating results.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article is a routine announcement of earnings release and conference call scheduling. There is no indication of positive or negative performance, financial guidance, or material developments. It is a standard procedural disclosure.

title: Texas Capital Bancshares, Inc. Announces Quarterly Dividend for Preferred Stock
author: Globe Newswire
description: Texas Capital Bancshares announced a cash dividend of $14.375 per share of its 5.75% Fixed Rate Non-Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock, payable on December 15, 2025, to shareholders of record as of December 1, 2025.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company reported record-level net income, declared a dividend, and demonstrated financial stability with presence in multiple Texas cities and national client network
